Now I have a complete picture. The ResponsesClient does NOT have its own CreatePipeline/CreateAddCustomHeadersPolicy methods - it relies on `OpenAIClient.CreatePipeline` and `OpenAIClient.GetEndpoint` which take `OpenAIClientOptions`. The RealtimeClient, in contrast, has its own `CreatePipeline` and `CreateAddCustomHeadersPolicy` that take `RealtimeClientOptions`.

However, looking more carefully, I see that the ResponsesClient constructors that take `OpenAIClientOptions` delegate to `OpenAIClient.CreatePipeline` and `OpenAIClient.GetEndpoint` (which take `OpenAIClientOptions`). I need to:

1. Create `ResponsesClientOptions` following the `RealtimeClientOptions` pattern
2. Add a `FromClientOptions` static method for conversion
3. Update `ResponsesClient` constructors to accept `ResponsesClientOptions`
4. Add private CreatePipeline/CreateAddCustomHeadersPolicy methods to `ResponsesClient`
5. Update `ResponsesClientSettings`
6. Update `OpenAIClient.GetResponsesClient()`

content.content:
970	### Secure Access with Microsoft Entra ID (No API Keys)
971	The starter kit includes examples showing how to call Azure OpenAI securely using Microsoft Entra ID instead of API keys. This is the recommended approach for production scenarios. Here’s a direct link to the .NET sample using Entra ID in the starter kit: https://github.com/Azure-Samples/azure-openai-starter/blob/main/src/dotnet/responses_example_entra.cs
972	
973	Below is the core pattern using the OpenAI SDK for .NET with Azure OpenAI + Entra ID:
974	
975	```C# Snippet:ReadMe_AzureOpenAI
976	var endpoint = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
977	    ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT is required.");
978	
979	var client = new ResponsesClient(
980	    new BearerTokenPolicy(new DefaultAzureCredential(), "https://ai.azure.com/.default"),
981	    new OpenAIClientOptions { Endpoint = new Uri($"{endpoint}/openai/v1/") }
982	);
983	
984	var response = await client.CreateResponseAsync("gpt-5-mini", "Hello world!");
985	Console.WriteLine(response.Value.GetOutputText());
986	```
987	
988	### Why this works
989	
990	- One OpenAI SDK: You use the official OpenAI SDK for .NET. Azure OpenAI is just a different endpoint you point the client library to.
991	- Unified /openai/v1/ endpoint: Azure OpenAI uses the same path shape as OpenAI, so most client code can stay unchanged.
992	- Enterprise-ready auth: Azure Identity SDK with Microsoft Entra ID lets you access Azure OpenAI without storing secrets.
993	- Drop‑in model switching: Swap "gpt-5-mini" or any other model as long as the Azure model deployment has the same name as the model.
994	
995	## Advanced scenarios
996	
997	### Using protocol methods
998	
999	In addition to the client methods that use strongly-typed request and response objects, the .NET library also provides _protocol methods_ that enable more direct access to the REST API. Protocol methods are "binary in, binary out" accepting `BinaryContent` as request bodies and providing `BinaryData` as response bodies.

## Summary of Changes

### Bug Description
The `ResponsesClient` constructors and `ResponsesClientSettings.Options` were using `OpenAIClientOptions`, even though the client itself lives in the `OpenAI.Responses` namespace. A dedicated `ResponsesClientOptions` was needed to make the API more consistent and better scoped to the Responses feature area.

### Files Changed

1. **`/testbed/src/Custom/Responses/ResponsesClientOptions.cs`** (NEW) - Added a dedicated `ResponsesClientOptions` class following the same pattern as `RealtimeClientOptions`, with:
   - `Endpoint`, `OrganizationId`, `ProjectId`, `UserAgentApplicationId` properties
   - Default constructor
   - Constructor from `IConfigurationSection` for configuration binding
   - Internal `FromClientOptions(OpenAIClientOptions)` static method for conversion/compatibility

2. **`/testbed/src/Custom/Responses/ResponsesClient.cs`** - Updated all constructors to use `ResponsesClientOptions` instead of `OpenAIClientOptions`:
   - All public constructors now accept `ResponsesClientOptions`
   - Added localized `CreatePipeline`, `GetEndpoint`, and `CreateAddCustomHeadersPolicy` methods that work with `ResponsesClientOptions`
   - Settings-based constructor now passes `ResponsesClientOptions.FromClientOptions(settings?.Options)`

3. **`/testbed/src/Custom/Responses/ResponsesClientSettings.cs`** - Changed `Options` property type from `OpenAIClientOptions` to `ResponsesClientOptions`. Updated `BindCore` to use the new `ResponsesClientOptions(IConfigurationSection)` constructor.

4. **`/testbed/src/Custom/OpenAIClient.cs`** - Updated `GetResponsesClient()` to convert `_options` using `ResponsesClientOptions.FromClientOptions(_options)`.

5. **`/testbed/tests/Utility/OpenAITestEnvironment.cs`** - Updated test client factory to use `ResponsesClientOptions` via a new `CreateResponsesClientOptions` helper (same pattern as the existing `CreateRealtimeClientOptions`).

6. **`/testbed/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs`** - Updated snippet to use `ResponsesClientOptions`.

7. **`/testbed/README.md`** - Updated documentation example to use `ResponsesClientOptions`.
